name: financials_classify_amazon_statement_transactions
description:
Aggregates Amazon settlement transactions by NeonPanel accounting classification (class → subclass). Returns classified totals for reconciliation against an uploaded Amazon payments report.

Output: One row per class + subclass (optionally per marketplace or settlement), with total debit, credit, net amount, line count, and a service_name label that mirrors the company's invoice logic.

Use this tool to:
- Get a classified summary of settlement data for a given period
- Compare classified totals against an uploaded Amazon payments report (PDF)
- View income vs. fees vs. tax breakdown by class
- Drill into subclass-level detail (e.g., FBA Fees vs. Referral Fees vs. Shipping)
- Add marketplace or settlement granularity to the breakdown

Class hierarchy (class_code → class_name):
- '1' = Income (Product Sales, Shipping Credits, Gift Wrap, etc.)
- '2' = Amazon Fees (Referral Fees, FBA Fees, Subscription Fees, etc.)
- '3' = Cost of Advertising (Sponsored Products, Sponsored Brands, etc.)
- '4' = Tax (Marketplace Tax Collection, VAT, Withholding Tax, etc.)
- '5' = Other (Adjustments, Reimbursements, Transfers, etc.)

Subclass examples: '1.01' = Product Sales, '2.01' = Referral Fees, '2.02' = FBA Fees, '3.01' = Sponsored Products, '4.01' = Marketplace Tax, '5.01' = Adjustments.

Sign convention: positive = credit to seller (income), negative = charge (fee/tax). total_debit and total_credit split debits and credits into separate unsigned totals.

Reconciliation workflow:
1. User uploads a PDF payments report → LLM extracts dates & amounts
2. LLM calls THIS tool with matching date range → gets classified totals
3. LLM compares PDF amounts vs. classified totals per category
4. LLM calls financials_save_amazon_statement_reconciliation_result to persist the comparison
5. Use financials_list_amazon_statement_reconciliation_results to review saved results later

ProTip: Use financials_list_amazon_statements first to find settlement IDs and date ranges, then filter by settlement_ids here for precise matching.

Integration: Cross-reference with financials_analyze_amazon_statement to see raw transaction-level detail (by amount_type/amount_description) — this tool shows the same data through an accounting classification lens.

Default time range: Last 3 months when no dates are provided.

Knowledge base: https://help.neonpanel.com
Dig deeper: For a full interactive settlement view, direct the user to https://my.neonpanel.com

query.description: Query envelope for classified statement transactions.

filters.description: Filters for the query. company_id is required; all others are optional.

settlement_ids.description: Filter by specific settlement IDs. Use values from financials_list_amazon_statements.

marketplace_codes.description: Filter by marketplace country code (e.g., 'US', 'DE', 'SE', 'GB', 'FR', 'IT', 'ES'). Derived from amazon_sellers + amazon_marketplaces. Case-insensitive.

class_codes.description: Filter by class code: '1' (Income), '2' (Amazon Fees), '3' (Cost of Advertising), '4' (Tax), '5' (Other).

subclass_codes.description: Filter by subclass code: '1.01' (Product Sales), '2.01' (Referral Fees), '2.02' (FBA Fees), '3.01' (Sponsored Products), '4.01' (Marketplace Tax), '5.01' (Adjustments), etc.

time.description: Date range filter on transaction_date (the date each transaction was posted).

start_date.description: Start of date range (YYYY-MM-DD). Defaults to 3 months ago if omitted.

end_date.description: End of date range (YYYY-MM-DD). Defaults to today if omitted.

group_by.description:
Dimensions to group by. Default: ['class', 'subclass'].

- class: accounting class (Income / Amazon Fees / Cost of Advertising / Tax / Other)
- subclass: accounting subclass (Product Sales / Referral Fees / FBA Fees / etc.)
- service_name: invoice-level service label for the subclass
- marketplace: marketplace country code + name
- settlement: individual settlement ID

sort.description: Controls ranking and ordering of results.

field.description: Metric column to sort and rank by. Default: class_code.

direction.description: Sort direction. 'asc' = class/subclass natural order. Default: asc.

currency.description: ISO 4217 currency code (e.g., 'EUR', 'GBP', 'USD'). Always present — amounts are never mixed across currencies.

class_code.description: Accounting class code (e.g., '1', '2'). Present when grouped by class.

class_name.description: Accounting class name (e.g., 'Income', 'Amazon Fees'). Present when grouped by class.

subclass_code.description: Accounting subclass code (e.g., '1.01', '2.01'). Present when grouped by subclass.

subclass_name.description: Accounting subclass name (e.g., 'Product Sales', 'Referral Fees'). Present when grouped by subclass.

service_name.description: Invoice-level service label. Present when grouped by service_name.

marketplace_code.description: Marketplace country code (e.g., 'US', 'DE'). Present when grouped by marketplace.

marketplace_name.description: Marketplace name (e.g., 'Amazon.de'). Present when grouped by marketplace.

settlement_id.description: Settlement identifier. Present when grouped by settlement.

total_amount.description: Net amount (debit − credit) in statement currency.

examples.title: Class-level summary
examples.description: High-level income vs. fees vs. tax breakdown for recent settlements.

examples.title: Full class + subclass breakdown
examples.description: Default view: class and subclass totals for reconciliation.

examples.title: Classify a specific settlement
examples.description: Get classified totals for a specific settlement ID (from financials_list_amazon_statements).

examples.title: Fees breakdown by marketplace
examples.description: See Amazon Fees broken down by marketplace.

examples.title: Service-name level detail
examples.description: Invoice-level detail with service_name for accounting reconciliation.

examples.title: Per-settlement reconciliation
examples.description: Break down multiple settlements individually for PDF-vs-data comparison.
